title: Derris robusta
taxonomy: Derris robusta is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It is commonly known by its synonyms, including Derris elliptica and Lonchocarpus robustus. Derris robusta is native to tropical Africa, and its classification places it within the genus Derris. The plant is a legume, which means it has the ability to fix nitrogen from the atmosphere, contributing to soil fertility. As a member of the Fabaceae family, Derris robusta shares characteristics with other legumes, including its seed pods and compound leaves.
uses: Derris robusta is primarily used as a medicinal plant in traditional medicine. It has been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and swelling in various parts of the body. The plant's extracts are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. In some cultures, it is also used as a treatment for snake bites and other venomous animal attacks. Its medicinal properties are still being studied and researched for potential uses in modern medicine.

constituents: Derris robusta contains a number of active constituents, including rotenone, derrone, and derricoline. These compounds have been found to have insecticidal properties, making Derris robusta a natural insecticide. Rotenone, in particular, has been shown to be highly effective against a wide range of insects, including mosquitoes and flies. side_effects: Derris robusta is known to be highly toxic, and its side effects can be severe if not handled properly. Ingestion of any part of the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Skin contact with the plant's sap can lead to irritation, redness, and blistering. Long-term exposure to Derris robusta has been linked to neurological damage and other systemic health issues. Due to its toxicity, handling the plant requires proper protective gear and caution.
intro: Derris robusta, also known as the robust derris, is a perennial legume native to tropical Australia and Southeast Asia. The robust derris is valued for its medicinal properties, which include potential health benefits in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The bioactive constituents of Derris robusta include rotenone and other rotenoids, which are responsible for its insecticidal and medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Derris robusta is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in the form of a tincture or extract, and is also used in some regions as a fish poison.
